item.title: Optimisation before Crohn's surgery using Exclusive enteral Nutrition (OCEaN): A randomised controlled trial to determine whether preoperative exclusive enteral nutrition is more clinically and cost effective compared with standard care in patients undergoing surgery for Crohn's disease.
item.description: Colorectal disease : the official journal of the Association of Coloproctology of Great Britain and Ireland - Exclusive enteral nutrition (EEN) is commonly used in paediatric Crohn's disease patients as a first-line therapy for inducing remission. Despite advances in immunosuppressive and biologic treatmen...

item.title: A New approach and conceptual model for understanding systemic sclerosis-associated gastrointestinal symptoms.
item.description: Expert review of clinical immunology - Review - Our understanding of the pathogenesis of systemic sclerosis-associated gastrointestinal (SSc-GI) disease is limited. This has hindered progress in the management of SSc-GI disease as objective meas...

item.title: Natural Broccoli Sprout-Derived Exosomes Encapsulating Bioactive Molecules as a Novel Nanomedicine for Repair of Intestinal Inflammation.
item.description: The AAPS journal - Dietary bioactives from broccoli sprouts exhibit anti-inflammatory properties in the prevention and management of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), but are limited by instability during gastrointes...

item.title: Dietary total antioxidant capacity alleviates inflammatory bowel disease-related surgery, gastrointestinal cancer, and mortality risks among middle-aged and older individuals.
item.description: The journal of nutrition, health & aging - This study aimed to investigate the association between dietary total antioxidant capacity (TAC) and the risks of clinical outcomes, including surgery, gastrointestinal cancer, and mortality, among...
